title: Voluspa 1-8: The Creation of the World
description: The Norse creation myth: from the void of Ginnungagap to the making of the world from Ymir's body.
introduction: The Norse creation myth: from the void of Ginnungagap to the making of the world from Ymir's body.

translation.original_text: Hljóðs bið ek allar helgar kindir, meiri ok minni, mögu Heimdallar. Viltu at ek, Valföðr, vel fyr telja forn spjöll fira, þau er fremst of man.
translation.text: Hearing I ask from the holy races, from Heimdall's sons, both high and low. You wish, Allfather, that I well relate the ancient tales I remember from the first.

translation.original_text: Ár var alda þat er ekki var, vara sandr né sær né svalar unnir; jörð fannsk æva né upphiminn, gap var ginnunga, en gras hvergi.
translation.text: In the beginning there was nothing: no sand, no sea, no cold waves; no earth, no sky above — only the yawning void, and nowhere grass.
